channel.title: Leave Management on Ventryx – Visitor & Attendance Management Software India
channel.description: Recent content in Leave Management on Ventryx – Visitor & Attendance Management Software India

item.title: The Complete Guide to Leave Management Systems for Indian Businesses
item.description:
Ask any HR manager at a mid-sized Indian company how they track employee leaves, and you will likely get one of two answers: “We use an Excel sheet” or “We have a WhatsApp group for that.” Both work - until they stop working. And they always stop working.

Whether you are running a 50-person startup in Pune or managing HR for a 500-employee manufacturing firm in Coimbatore, leave management is one of those invisible problems that quietly drains productivity, creates payroll headaches, and causes more interpersonal friction than almost any other workplace issue. An employee thinks they applied for Diwali leave. The manager thinks they approved it. Payroll thinks it was unpaid. Nobody is wrong, but everyone is frustrated.

item.title: Leave Types in India Explained: CL, EL, SL, Comp-Off and How to Manage Them All
item.description:
If you have ever tried to explain your company’s leave policy to a new employee and watched their eyes glaze over halfway through, you are not alone. Leave management in India is genuinely complex - not because companies make it so deliberately, but because the underlying legal framework layers central legislation on top of state-specific rules, and most organisations add their own policies on top of that.

The result is that even experienced HR professionals sometimes get the rules wrong. Casual leave and earned leave get conflated. Encashment is offered when it should not be, or denied when it should be mandatory. Carryover limits are set informally and then disputed when an employee tries to claim them.
